rationale: Type mismatches indicate logic errors; Redox enforces strict Hindley-Milner type unification
pattern: Unify(A, B) where A != B and !coercible(A, B)
fix_template: Convert the value to the expected type using an explicit conversion

description: Integer arithmetic may overflow at runtime
rationale: Unchecked integer overflow can cause panics in debug mode or wrap around in release mode
pattern: Add(x: T, y: T) where T: integer and !checked
alternatives: Use wrapping arithmetic if overflow is intentional

description: Numeric cast may lose precision or truncate
rationale: Implicit lossy casts hide data corruption; Redox requires explicit @cast for narrowing conversions
pattern: Cast(x: T, U) where sizeof(U) < sizeof(T) or signed(T) != signed(U)
fix_template: Use `@cast(x, U)` to make the narrowing explicit, or `x.try_into()` for a checked conversion

description: u32→usize is lossless on 32-bit and 64-bit targets
rationale: usize is always >= 32 bits; u32→usize never loses data

description: Option value used without checking for None
rationale: Accessing the inner value of a None causes a panic; all Option values must be explicitly matched or unwrapped safely
fix_template: Use pattern matching: `? opt { Some(v) => use(v), None => handle() }`

description: Result value used without checking for Err
rationale: Ignoring errors hides failures; all Result values must be matched, propagated (?), or explicitly discarded
fix_template: Propagate the error with `?` or handle it with pattern matching

description: Match expression does not cover all variants of the enum
rationale: Missing match arms cause runtime panics; Redox requires exhaustive pattern matching
fix_template: Add missing variants or use a wildcard `_ => ...` arm

description: Pattern in match arm is unreachable — previous arms already cover it
rationale: Unreachable patterns indicate dead code or logic errors in pattern ordering
pattern: Match { arm_i => ..., arm_j => ... } where arm_i subsumes arm_j, i < j
fix_template: Remove the unreachable arm or reorder patterns

description: Required trait not implemented for the type
rationale: Calling a method or using a type in a generic context requires the appropriate trait bound to be satisfied
pattern: Call(x.method()) where typeof(x): !impl Trait for method
fix_template: Implement the required trait: `I Trait for Type { ... }`
alternatives: Add a trait bound to the generic parameter

description: Type cannot be constructed — infinite recursion in type definition
rationale: A type that contains itself directly has infinite size; use indirection (^T, $T, @T) to break the cycle
alternatives: Use Rc for shared recursive structures

rationale: Unused variables may indicate dead code or missing logic
fix_template: Prefix with underscore: `v _x = ...` to indicate intentional discard

description: Struct literal is missing required field(s)
rationale: All struct fields must be initialized; uninitialized fields contain undefined memory
fix_template: Add the missing field(s) to the struct literal
alternatives: Use Default::default() for fields with a default value

description: Function performs an effect not declared in its signature
rationale: Effect inference verifies that declared effects are a superset of actual effects; undeclared effects violate the effect contract
fix_template: Add the effect to the function signature: `f foo() !{IO} { ... }`
alternatives: Handle the effect inside the function
